nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Millicent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Melisende, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from amal ‘labour’ + swinth ‘strength’. This was the name of a daughter of Charlemagne. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Millicent Hastings was born in 1145, in Fillongley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh de Hastings, was 47 and her mother, Erneburga Flamville, was 43. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Robert Foliot. She died in Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Millicent Hastingsが1145年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアフィロングリーで生まれた時，父Hugh de Hastingsは47歳，母Erneburga Flamvilleは43歳であった。彼女はRobert Foliotとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女はイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアチップイング ワーデンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Millicent Hastings(이)가 1145년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 피론글리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh de Hastings(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Erneburga Flamville(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 Robert Foliot(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 노르섬프토실르 치핀그 워든에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Millicent Hastings родилась в 1145 г. в местности филлонгли, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh de Hastings было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Erneburga Flamville, 43. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Robert Foliot. Она умерла в местности чиппинг варден, нортамптоншир, Англия.
